jokes.text: Jeff's overwrought TV commercial with dramatic music and grandiose claims
jokes.setup: Opening with ominous 'I am the system' voiceover suggests serious legal drama
jokes.punchline: Cut to cheesy local TV commercial with Jeff as 'hero at law' with parking promises
jokes.explanation: Strong character-specific parody that establishes Jeff's current desperate state through visual comedy. Well-structured contrast between dramatic opening and cheesy reality.

jokes.text: Maybe you should've spent less money on special effect.
jokes.setup: Jeff's commercial clearly had minimal budget and poor production values
jokes.punchline: Alan sarcastically suggests the opposite - that Jeff spent too much on effects
jokes.explanation: Decent sarcastic observation but fairly predictable. Competent but not inspired.

jokes.text: Huh, thanks a lot, Francis Ford repo man.
jokes.setup: Alan just criticized Jeff's commercial production quality
jokes.punchline: Jeff combines 'Francis Ford Coppola' with 'repo man' to create an insult about Alan's job
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that shows Jeff's wit even when desperate. Good economy and character voice.

jokes.text: Sad reprise of Jeff's commercial now showing 'Loser at law' in a mini mall
jokes.setup: We just saw Jeff's grandiose 'hero at law' commercial
jokes.punchline: Same format but now shows the pathetic reality - 'loser at law' in mini mall
jokes.explanation: Excellent structural callback that inverts the original commercial. Strong visual storytelling.

jokes.text: Relax, tango. I come in peace.
jokes.setup: Alan enters Jeff's pathetic office
jokes.punchline: Uses 'tango' as a casual address and alien contact phrase inappropriately
jokes.explanation: Mildly absurd character voice but not particularly clever or memorable.

jokes.text: A human could've said that without salivating.
jokes.setup: Alan enthusiastically describes the bridge collapse case
jokes.punchline: Jeff suggests Alan's excitement is inhuman/predatory
jokes.explanation: Sharp character observation with excellent economy. Captures Jeff's moral superiority and Alan's sleaziness.

jokes.text: From what? His own pencil?
jokes.setup: Alan says the engineer wants to seek damages
jokes.punchline: Jeff points out the absurdity - the engineer would be suing himself essentially
jokes.explanation: Excellent economy in pointing out logical flaw. Quick wit but fairly predictable.

jokes.text: Weird choice, by the way, the bachelor's degree on the wall.
jokes.setup: Alan is discussing suing Greendale
jokes.punchline: Casually insults Jeff's education choice while asking for help
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Alan's tactless nature, but not particularly funny.

jokes.text: Jeff, I once saw you convince an arson victim that he liked his house better burned.
jokes.setup: Discussion of Jeff's past legal skills
jokes.punchline: Absurdly dark example of Jeff's persuasive abilities
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly dark and specific example that perfectly captures Jeff's past ruthlessness. Highly quotable and memorable.

jokes.text: the next bone you get thrown might be through a dude's zipper in the warehouse district
jokes.setup: Alan threatening Jeff if he doesn't help
jokes.punchline: Euphemistic threat about prostitution work
jokes.explanation: Crude threat that's more uncomfortable than funny. Relies on shock value.

jokes.text: Look, I might be broke, desperate, and nearly homeless, but I still got one thing deep down inside that gives me power... That was it. That was all I had.
jokes.setup: Jeff builds up to revealing his secret source of power
jokes.punchline: Immediately deflates by admitting that was all he had
jokes.explanation: Excellent structure with perfect timing. Great character moment showing Jeff's self-awareness and defeat.

jokes.text: ranked America's number two community college by GreendaleCommunityCollege.Com
jokes.setup: Dean announces Greendale's ranking achievement
jokes.punchline: The ranking is from their own website, making it meaningless
jokes.explanation: Good character-specific absurdity showing the Dean's delusional optimism.

jokes.text: followed by four years of silence to the press
jokes.setup: Moment of silence for bridge tragedy
jokes.punchline: Escalates to ridiculously long period of press silence
jokes.explanation: Good escalation joke showing institutional panic and incompetence.

jokes.text: I told you you'd be back. Shut up, Leonard. I'm not here. I'm just a figment of your creeping dementia.
jokes.setup: Leonard smugly says he predicted Jeff's return
jokes.punchline: Jeff's elaborate dismissal suggesting Leonard is senile
jokes.explanation: Creative and mean comeback that's very Jeff-like. Good character interaction.

jokes.text: [Blows raspberry]
jokes.setup: Jeff delivers sophisticated insult about dementia
jokes.punchline: Leonard responds with childish raspberry sound
jokes.explanation: Simple physical comedy that works as character beat but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: I've always regretted not telling you how much you remind me of my aunt.
jokes.setup: Jeff tries to charm the receptionist for information
jokes.punchline: Weird aunt comparison as attempted flirtation
jokes.explanation: Awkward Jeff charm attempt that shows his desperation and rusty social skills.

jokes.text: It's more disarming than it sounds. I have a very attractive aunt.
jokes.setup: Jeff's weird aunt comment
jokes.punchline: Jeff doubles down with even weirder clarification
jokes.explanation: Continuation of awkward moment that makes it worse. Good cringe comedy.

jokes.text: You're, uh, Rita. - Rhonda.
jokes.setup: Jeff tries to use personal connection
jokes.punchline: Gets her name completely wrong
jokes.explanation: Very basic 'wrong name' gag. Predictable but functional for character moment.

jokes.text: Maybe you're looking for something that was in front of you the whole time just a pitch.
jokes.setup: Dean tries to be profound about Jeff's return
jokes.punchline: Immediately undercuts profundity by admitting it's just a pitch
jokes.explanation: Good self-aware humor showing Dean's manipulative but incompetent nature.

jokes.text: The Save Greendale Committee. - Working title.
jokes.setup: Dean announces grandiose committee name
jokes.punchline: Immediately undermines it as just a working title
jokes.explanation: Decent character moment showing Dean's lack of commitment to his own ideas.

jokes.text: And asbestos reasons, but it's clean as a whistle now.
jokes.setup: Study room closed for sentimental reasons
jokes.punchline: Casually mentions dangerous health hazard then dismisses it
jokes.explanation: Good dark humor about institutional negligence delivered with Dean's typical obliviousness.

jokes.text: I see your value now.
jokes.setup: Abed appears unexpectedly
jokes.punchline: References their first meeting from season 1
jokes.explanation: Strong callback that rewards long-time viewers and fits Abed's meta character perfectly.

jokes.text: And me calling years 'seasons' is a callback to my first season dialogue, and me calling...
jokes.setup: Abed explains his callback
jokes.punchline: Meta-explains his own meta behavior in infinite regress
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly meta moment that's quintessential Abed. Highly original and perfectly executed for the character.

jokes.text: Abed, I know what makes you off-putting, but why are you here?
jokes.setup: Abed being typically meta and verbose
jokes.punchline: Jeff cuts through with blunt acknowledgment of Abed's personality
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jeff response - direct, slightly mean, but efficient. Good character dynamic.

jokes.text: [Excited squealing and cheering] when the study group reunites
jokes.setup: Emotional reunion moment building
jokes.punchline: Over-the-top excited reactions suggesting they're all desperate
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy that works for character development but isn't particularly original.

jokes.text: Well, you guys just got sprung from this joint, and I figured you had careers to get started. - Yeah, working hard. - Ah, yes. [All talking over each other] [Conversation peters out]
jokes.setup: Jeff assumes they all have successful careers now
jokes.punchline: Awkward overlapping responses that fade to uncomfortable silence revealing failure
jokes.explanation: Well-structured cringe moment that efficiently reveals everyone's failure. Good use of silence.

jokes.text: This is where we became a family. [Chuckles] Jeff made us a family.
jokes.setup: Emotional moment about their study room
jokes.punchline: Troy's innocent delivery of something that sounds uncomfortably cult-like
jokes.explanation: Good character moment that's both sweet and slightly unsettling in its devotion.

jokes.text: We could repilot it. This could be like Scrubs season nine, a revamp, a do-over.
jokes.setup: Discussion of returning to Greendale
jokes.punchline: Abed frames it in TV terms, referencing actual show reboot
jokes.explanation: Excellent meta-joke that comments on the show's own situation. Very Abed-specific and clever.

jokes.text: I'm not gonna be a teacher, Abed. That's lame.
jokes.setup: Abed suggests Jeff become a teacher
jokes.punchline: Jeff dismisses teaching as lame despite being desperate and homeless
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Jeff's stubborn pride despite circumstances.

jokes.text: Zach Braff was only in the first six episodes. That son of a bitch. After everything scrubs did for him?
jokes.setup: Abed mentions Scrubs season 9
jokes.punchline: Troy takes personal offense at Zach Braff's limited involvement
jokes.explanation: Excellent character comedy showing Troy's innocent emotional investment in TV shows.

jokes.text: Okay! So sue me! This ain't Harvard, Jeffrey.
jokes.setup: Jeff discovering the Dean destroyed evidence
jokes.punchline: Dean's defensive outburst admitting institutional inadequacy
jokes.explanation: Good character breakdown moment showing Dean's defensive nature and the school's reality.

jokes.text: my job is to give a certain number of degrees per year, or we could get shut down. And that would be bad because... Ah, because then I wouldn't have a job.
jokes.setup: Dean trying to justify his actions
jokes.punchline: Realizes his motivation is purely selfish
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Dean's self-awareness breaking through his delusions.

jokes.text: My wife left. My girlfriend dumped me. And they're sisters, so now the whole family's pissed.
jokes.setup: Alan explaining his personal troubles
jokes.punchline: Reveals he was cheating with sisters, making it worse
jokes.explanation: Well-structured escalation that reveals character's sleaziness efficiently.

jokes.text: And that's the Winger guarantee.
jokes.setup: Jeff switching sides and creating new clients
jokes.punchline: Uses his commercial catchphrase in new context
jokes.explanation: Good callback that shows Jeff's return to form and manipulation skills.

jokes.text: Or opening a zoo where the humans are in cages and the animals come to visit.
jokes.setup: Discussing fundraising ideas that aren't bake sales
jokes.punchline: Britta's characteristically pretentious and impractical suggestion
jokes.explanation: Perfect Britta idea - well-intentioned but completely impractical and pseudo-profound.

jokes.text: That's like me blaming owls for how much I suck at analogies.
jokes.setup: Britta making an analogy about blame
jokes.punchline: Self-referential joke about her own bad analogy skills
jokes.explanation: Brilliant self-referential joke that's perfectly Britta. Excellent structure and highly quotable. Peak comedy writing.

jokes.text: I love hospital administration. - Is that your job? - Well...not yet. But I administer things to hospitals, like these. Pen? 'Futurza'?
jokes.setup: Annie claims to love her career in hospital administration
jokes.punchline: Reveals she just pushes pens for a pharmaceutical company
jokes.explanation: Good character development showing Annie's self-deception about her career disappointment.

jokes.text: They invented fibromyalgia and the cure for fibromyalgia.
jokes.setup: Annie describing her pharmaceutical company
jokes.punchline: Dark joke about creating diseases to sell cures
jokes.explanation: Sharp dark humor about pharmaceutical industry practices. Well-crafted and memorable.

jokes.text: You push drugs now? That's ironic.
jokes.setup: Annie working for pharmaceutical company
jokes.punchline: Reference to Annie's past drug problems and current reversal
jokes.explanation: Sharp callback to character history with good irony. Efficient and character-specific.

jokes.text: You're jewish?
jokes.setup: Britta mentions being on sabbatical
jokes.punchline: Jeff misunderstands sabbatical as religious rather than career break
jokes.explanation: Simple misunderstanding joke that works for character moment but isn't particularly clever.

jokes.text: You're a bartender, aren't you? Which is basically like a modern-day therapist. If somebody tells me about a murder, I can't tell anyone.
jokes.setup: Jeff guesses Britta's real job
jokes.punchline: Britta's completely wrong understanding of bartender-client privilege
jokes.explanation: Excellent Britta moment showing her pseudo-intellectual misunderstanding. Very quotable and character-perfect.

jokes.text: Wow, you're even bad at bartending.
jokes.setup: Britta's confidentiality confusion
jokes.punchline: Jeff's cutting assessment of her competence
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jeff comeback - economical and cutting. Good character dynamics.

jokes.text: I didn't like the number. 555? It's derivative. It sounds fake. How can every number start with 555? Are there only 10,000 numbers?
jokes.setup: Abed explaining why he quit directing Jeff's commercial
jokes.punchline: Refuses to use fake TV numbers for artistic integrity reasons
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-joke about TV conventions delivered perfectly in character. Very Abed and highly original.

jokes.text: Well, why don't you sell him a pill that will help him give up on his dreams?
jokes.setup: Tension between Annie and Abed about money
jokes.punchline: Britta's dark suggestion about pharmaceutical solutions to life problems
jokes.explanation: Sharp dark humor that's both funny and pointed social commentary. Good character voice.

jokes.text: Relaxabrex doesn't make you give up on your dreams. That's a side effect.
jokes.setup: Britta's suggestion about dream-crushing pills
jokes.punchline: Annie's defensive clarification makes it worse by admitting it's real
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation that makes the dark joke even darker. Great pharmaceutical satire.

jokes.text: At least the drugs I sell don't get slurped out of my belly button. That's only on Tummy Tuesdays!
jokes.setup: Argument about their respective degrading jobs
jokes.punchline: Troy's cheerful clarification about disgusting work detail
jokes.explanation: Good gross-out humor that shows Troy's innocent attitude toward degrading work.

jokes.text: 166 episodes of Bones just gone.
jokes.setup: Shirley listing what her husband took
jokes.punchline: Treats recorded TV shows as equally important as family and pets
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing misplaced priorities in grief. Relatable and specific.

jokes.text: I cheated on him with Shirley's Sandwiches.
jokes.setup: Shirley admitting fault in marriage problems
jokes.punchline: Frames business investment as infidelity
jokes.explanation: Clever metaphor that reframes business failure as relationship betrayal. Good character voice.

jokes.text: Classic Winger.
jokes.setup: Jeff reveals his manipulation scheme
jokes.punchline: Alan recognizes Jeff's signature move pattern
jokes.explanation: Simple recognition moment that works for character dynamics but isn't particularly funny.

jokes.text: You trust-funded, body-sprayed, Eli-Roth-DVD owner...
jokes.setup: Jeff turning on Alan
jokes.punchline: Increasingly specific and insulting character assassination
jokes.explanation: Good character-specific insults that feel authentic to Jeff's voice and Alan's type.

jokes.text: You're not worth the monologue. Give me your tie.
jokes.setup: Jeff building to dramatic speech
jokes.punchline: Self-aware interruption of his own dramatic moment
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-moment that subverts dramatic convention while being perfectly in character. Highly quotable.

jokes.text: your tears are just ones and zeroes to me.
jokes.setup: Group sharing emotional problems
jokes.punchline: Abed's computer-influenced emotional detachment
jokes.explanation: Perfect Abed line that combines his tech background with emotional distance. Very quotable.

jokes.text: Yeah, you were a real Hallmark card before.
jokes.setup: Abed claiming computer work made him less emotional
jokes.punchline: Jeff points out Abed was never emotional to begin with
jokes.explanation: Good sarcastic comeback that's true to character dynamics. Solid but not exceptional.

jokes.text: I'm much sadder than the rest of you. [Sniffling] I will figure out why later.
jokes.setup: Group sadness competition
jokes.punchline: Abed's analytical approach to his own emotions
jokes.explanation: Perfect Abed moment treating emotions like a technical problem to solve later.

jokes.text: Chang? - Kevin?
jokes.setup: Unexpected Chang appearance
jokes.punchline: Reference to Chang's fake amnesia identity
jokes.explanation: Good callback for longtime viewers but not particularly funny on its own.

jokes.text: Math teacher. - explain the pajamas. - I don't have a home. See this? This won't let me travel 100 yards from this, and this only has a 30-yard cord, so I can only travel... Whatever. You do the math. - You said you teach math. - Exactly. I teach it. You do it.
jokes.setup: Chang's bizarre work/living situation
jokes.punchline: Chang's refusal to do math despite being a math teacher
jokes.explanation: Excellent Chang moment showing his lazy logic and the school's dysfunction. Very quotable.

jokes.text: After being rehired as a security guard after being fired for impersonating a teacher. That's insane, and I'm Abed.
jokes.setup: Chang's employment history at Greendale
jokes.punchline: Abed using himself as the baseline for insanity recognition
jokes.explanation: Brilliant self-aware joke where Abed acknowledges his own weirdness to emphasize how crazy something else is.

jokes.text: But this school clearly got a finger up its butt as a child.
jokes.setup: Discussion of the school's problems
jokes.punchline: Britta's pseudo-psychological diagnosis using crude imagery
jokes.explanation: Very Britta attempt at psychology that's crude and inappropriate. Character-appropriate but not brilliant.

jokes.text: I'm gonna go score some blow. My dealer's a gym teacher here.
jokes.setup: Alan leaving the scene
jokes.punchline: Casually mentions buying drugs from school staff
jokes.explanation: Adds to institutional corruption theme but relies mainly on shock value.

jokes.text: Britta, when we met, you were an eclectic anarchist. How did you become the group's airhead?
jokes.setup: Jeff's manipulation speech to each character
jokes.punchline: Direct acknowledgment of Britta's character evolution/degradation
jokes.explanation: Meta-commentary on character flanderization that's both funny and self-aware.

jokes.text: Thank you?
jokes.setup: Jeff calling her an airhead
jokes.punchline: Britta's confused gratitude
jokes.explanation: Perfect Britta response showing her confusion about whether insults are compliments.

jokes.text: Troy, your entire identity has been consumed by your relationship with another man. You found my Clive Owen Tumblr.
jokes.setup: Jeff criticizing Troy's relationship dependency
jokes.punchline: Troy thinks this is about discovering his secret celebrity crush blog
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection that subverts expectation while being perfectly Troy-like. Very quotable.

jokes.text: Oh, don't blame it all on the gas leak year.
jokes.setup: Annie mentions the gas leak year excuse
jokes.punchline: Meta-reference to the show's actual bad season 4
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-joke addressing the show's real-world production problems. Very clever and self-aware.

jokes.text: You staged a robot fight?
jokes.setup: Jeff admitting the commercial was fake
jokes.punchline: Annie focuses on the least important fabrication detail
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Annie's priorities, though not particularly funny.

jokes.text: Yeah. In real life, the robot wins.
jokes.setup: Annie's question about the robot fight
jokes.punchline: Jeff's pessimistic view of technology vs humanity
jokes.explanation: Good dark humor that fits Jeff's defeated state and world view.

jokes.text: The same reason I never returned Time Bandits to Blockbuster. Nobody else deserves it.
jokes.setup: Jeff wanting to burn the study table
jokes.punchline: Compares to petty theft justified by perceived superiority
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jeff moment - petty, elitist, and self-justifying. Good specific cultural reference.

jokes.text: Let's just say some women can't take a compliment.
jokes.setup: Pierce mentions court order about gender sensitivity
jokes.punchline: Characteristically tone-deaf assessment of sexual harassment
jokes.explanation: Very Pierce-like comment that's both awful and perfectly in character.

jokes.text: only because it gives crappy people a chance to sort themselves out.
jokes.setup: Pierce defending Greendale as a crappy place
jokes.punchline: Backhanded compliment that includes self-awareness
jokes.explanation: Good Pierce moment that's both insulting and oddly touching in its honesty.

jokes.text: Did I sound gay at the end? Do you want to do another take?
jokes.setup: Pierce finishing his heartfelt message
jokes.punchline: Breaks character to worry about seeming gay, reveals it's recorded
jokes.explanation: Excellent meta-moment that breaks the fourth wall while being perfectly Pierce-like in its homophobia.

jokes.text: It's my whole ideantity! I mean, wah, see what I did there? That's proof!
jokes.setup: Dean explaining why he can't stop being Dean
jokes.punchline: Makes terrible pun then proudly points it out
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dean moment - bad pun delivered with pride. Very character-specific humor.

jokes.text: You just described slavery.
jokes.setup: Dean offers job with no money
jokes.punchline: Jeff's blunt assessment of unpaid labor
jokes.explanation: Direct and economical Jeff response that cuts through Dean's obliviousness.

jokes.text: Screw you, Abed.
jokes.setup: Jeff accepting teaching job
jokes.punchline: Blames Abed for his accurate prediction/manipulation
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing Jeff's grudging acceptance of Abed's prescience.

jokes.text: See, he does a voice-over wrap-up at the end of every episode. - Wow. - Cool.
jokes.setup: Jeff doing typical sitcom wrap-up voiceover
jokes.punchline: Abed points out the TV convention while it's happening
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-joke that breaks the fourth wall while the convention is actively happening. Peak Community humor.

jokes.text: Use that. - I want to kill myself. - Use that.
jokes.setup: Dean practicing being more teacherly
jokes.punchline: Jeff encouraging Dean to use his suicidal ideation as teaching motivation
jokes.explanation: Dark humor that shows Jeff's pragmatic approach to using personal trauma for motivation.

jokes.text: [Farts] [Laughs]
jokes.setup: End credits scene with Chang
jokes.punchline: Chang farts and laughs at his own fart
jokes.explanation: Basic toilet humor that's childish even for Chang. Lazy comedy but somewhat in character.
